an3.2:1.1: “Monks, a fool is characterized by his/her actions. A wise person is characterized by his/her actions. It is through the activities of one’s life that one’s discernment shines.
an3.2:1.2: “A person endowed with three things is to be recognized as a fool.
an3.2:1.4: Bodily misconduct, verbal misconduct, mental misconduct.
an3.2:1.5: A person endowed with these three things is to be recognized as a fool.
an3.2:2.1: “A person endowed with three things is to be recognized as a wise person.
an3.2:2.3: Good bodily conduct, good verbal conduct, good mental conduct.
an3.2:2.4: A person endowed with these three things is to be recognized as a wise person.
an3.2:3.2: ‘We will avoid the three things that, endowed with which, one is to be recognized as a fool. We will undertake & maintain the three things that, endowed with which, one is to be recognized as a wise person.’
an3.2:3.3: That’s how you should train yourselves.”
